talk_metadata.session_title: Therapeutic Advances in Antibody Drug Conjugates
talk_metadata.talk_title: CTEP Trial Number 10355: Phase 1 trial of DS-8201A and olaparib in HER2-expressing malignancies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "All pneumonitis events were low grade and occurred across dosing schedules, tumor types, and levels of HER2 expression."
Speaker said: "We observed a confirmed complete response and 12 confirmed partial responses."
Speaker said: "The majority of patients had wild-type BRCA by local germline and somatic testing."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Hematologic toxicity not fully mitigated despite gap schedule
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Confirmed complete response and partial responses observed in a heavily pretreated cohort
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Grade 3/4 neutropenia, anemia, and thrombocytopenia were markedly attenuated but still present

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: HER2-targeted TOP1 inhibitor payload combined with PARP inhibition
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled HER2 IHC 1+ patients without biomarker enrichment, potentially diluting signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No stratification by BRCA mutation or HRD status
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Single-arm dose escalation, no comparator arm

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on HER2 expression; activity seen even at HER2 IHC 1+ suggesting limited specificity
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Combination with PARP inhibitor adds hematologic toxicity, limiting broader use

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed deep tumor reductions across HER2 low (IHC 1+) patients, then concluded that the regimen is broadly feasible without biomarker selection

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of HRD/BRCA status stratification limits ability to identify patients who may benefit most from PARP inhibition
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted only one patient was BRCA mutant and did not analyze outcomes by BRCA status
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO could integrate genomic HRD scores to select patients with synthetic lethality potential

crispro_opportunity.description: Staggered schedule reduces overlapping myelosuppression, suggesting potential to combine with other agents that have non-overlapping toxicities
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Discussant highlighted reduced hematologic AEs with gap schedule olaparib
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model optimal sequencing windows for ADC and targeted agents

unresolved_questions: Why do some HER2 IHC 1+ patients achieve deep responses while others do not?
What is the optimal duration of olaparib dosing to balance efficacy and toxicity?

talk_metadata.session_title: Therapeutic Advances in Antibody Drug Conjugates
talk_metadata.talk_title: First-in-human study of SOS 6010, an EGFR-targeted ADC in advanced nasopharyngeal carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The OR is 31.5% and the 4.8 milligram per kilogram group achieved a higher OR of 36.4%"
Speaker said: "Most common Grade 3 TEAEs were hematological toxicity"
Speaker said: "No clear signal was observed between EGFR expression and the response rate"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Hematologic toxicity inherent to TOP1 payload not fully mitigated by linker design
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Higher OR in EGFR monoclonal antibody‑naïve subgroup
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Grade 3/4 hematologic events in 61% of patients

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Allowed enrollment regardless of EGFR expression level, potentially diluting efficacy signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No requirement for high EGFR IHC or quantitative EGFR expression

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: EGFR ADC with cleavable linker and TOP1 payload
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed high DAR and cleavable linker, which are shared features across many EGFR ADCs
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: High hematologic toxicity may limit combination possibilities

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented high ORR in EGFR‑naïve patients but concluded that EGFR expression level does not affect response

crispro_opportunity.description: Absence of EGFR expression‑based selection; data suggest better responses in EGFR‑naïve patients
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted higher ORR in EGFR monoclonal antibody‑naïve subgroup
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop predictive models integrating EGFR IHC, prior EGFR‑mAb exposure, and genomic data

crispro_opportunity.description: High hematologic toxicity suggests need for non‑overlapping partners such as immune checkpoint inhibitors with distinct safety profiles
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Discussant mentioned ongoing combination with PD‑1 inhibitor
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate synergistic schedules minimizing overlapping myelosuppression

cited_competitors.name: Trastuzumab deruxtecan
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as comparator ADC with HER2 target

unresolved_questions: Why do EGFR‑naïve patients show higher response despite EGFR being the target?
Can a more stable linker reduce hematologic toxicity?

talk_metadata.session_title: Therapeutic Advances in Antibody Drug Conjugates
talk_metadata.talk_title: First-in-human Phase 1 trial of QLS-5132, a claudin‑6 targeting ADC in platinum‑resistant ovarian cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We observed one DLT ... Grade 4 platelet count decrease, fully recovered to baseline"
Speaker said: "ORR was 55.6% at the 4.8 mg/kg dose"
Speaker said: "No significant correlation between CLDN6 expression and response was seen"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of correlation between CLDN6 expression and response suggests target may not be primary driver
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Two patients with undetectable CLDN6 achieved PR
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Grade 3/4 hematologic toxicities in 32.1% of patients

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled all comers with PROC regardless of CLDN6 expression, potentially diluting efficacy
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No pre‑selection for high CLDN6 expression
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small sample size (28 patients) without control arm

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Claudin‑6 ADC with hydrazonic linker and TOP1 payload
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Linker and payload are similar to other TOP1 ADCs, offering limited differentiation
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Efficacy appears dose‑dependent but target expression not predictive, limiting biomarker‑driven development

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker reported high ORR (55%) yet admitted no correlation between CLDN6 expression and response

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a predictive model for response beyond CLDN6 expression, incorporating genomic and tumor microenvironment features
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted lack of correlation between CLDN6 levels and response
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omics to identify hidden predictive signatures

crispro_opportunity.description: Two CLDN6‑negative responders suggest alternative uptake mechanisms that could be exploited
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Two patients with undetectable CLDN6 achieved PR
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model off‑target internalization pathways for ADCs

unresolved_questions: What drives response in CLDN6‑negative tumors?
Would a biomarker‑selected cohort improve ORR and safety?

talk_metadata.session_title: Therapeutic Advances in Antibody Drug Conjugates
talk_metadata.talk_title: ARTEMIS‑101: Phase 1 study of REVSYBOO (tagretucumab) plus atezolizumab in pre‑treated advanced non‑squamous NSCLC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The combination achieved a confirmed ORR of 47.1% and a median PFS of 14 months"
Speaker said: "Grade 3 or higher TRAEs occurred in 70% of the patients, predominantly hematological toxicities"
Speaker said: "Efficacy was consistent across B7‑H3 expression levels and independent of prior immunotherapy exposure"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: High hematologic toxicity (70% grade ≥3) not fully mitigated by schedule
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Patients with prior immunotherapy still achieved responses
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Grade 3/4 hematologic events in 70% of cohort

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: B7‑H3 targeting TOP1 inhibitor ADC combined with PD‑L1 blockade
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Mixed PD‑L1 expression and prior immunotherapy exposure without stratification, potentially confounding efficacy assessment
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No prospective selection based on B7‑H3 intensity
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small expansion cohort (34 efficacy evaluable) without control arm

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: B7‑H3 ADC with tropomodulin‑1 inhibitor payload
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Payload and linker are shared among several B7‑H3 ADCs in development, limiting IP moat
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: High hematologic toxicity may limit combination with other myelosuppressive agents

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker reported consistent efficacy across B7‑H3 expression levels yet emphasized need for biomarker‑driven development

key_data_gaps: Limited data on PD‑L1 negative subgroup

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of B7‑H3 expression‑based selection despite variable response
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted efficacy across B7‑H3 levels
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model quantitative B7‑H3 scoring to identify high‑benefit subpopulations

crispro_opportunity.description: High hematologic toxicity suggests exploring non‑myelosuppressive partners (e.g., targeted agents) to improve tolerability
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Discussant highlighted need for biomarker‑selected trials
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate safety profiles of alternative combos

unresolved_questions: Does B7‑H3 expression truly predict benefit, or is response driven by PD‑L1 status?
What is the durability of response in PD‑L1 negative patients?
